title: Japan loses 1-2 to Brazil in World Cup round of 16
summary: The Japanese national team was eliminated by Brazil in the first knockout round, extending their winless streak in the elimination stage.

title: Yen hits 39-and-a-half-year low against dollar, approaching 162 yen
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend due to interest rate differences, fueling expectations of government intervention.

title: U.S. Supreme Court rules against Trump's removal of a Federal Reserve board member
summary: The ruling reaffirms the central bank's independence by considering the dismissal of a Fed member invalid.

title: Shooting at a youth center in northern Germany leaves at least five dead
summary: Two suspects were arrested after the attack at a youth care facility in the state of Schleswig-Holstein.

title: 29-year-old man arrested for murder of a person buried alive in Hiroshima
summary: The suspect, an acquaintance of the victim, allegedly killed to cover up another crime, according to police.

title: Idol Kazuya Kamenashi and presenter Minami Tanaka announce marriage and pregnancy
summary: The couple confirmed the news through joint statements, sparking excitement among their fans.

title: Train derails at Kyoto Station on the Kintetsu line with no injuries
summary: The incident affected about 85,000 passengers and caused the suspension of service between Kyoto and Kami-Toba.

title: High school student drowns off Niigata coast
summary: The youth was swept away by the wind while fishing on a breakwater and could not return to shore.

title: 139 children suffer food poisoning at a Tokyo elementary school
summary: An outbreak of gastroenteritis, likely caused by norovirus, led to mass absences at the school.

title: Couple accused of instigating four youths to commit robbery and murder in Tochigi
summary: The suspects allegedly gave instructions to minors to carry out the crime using a screwdriver.

title: China expands export restrictions against 20 Japanese companies
summary: The Chinese government added new companies to its list of banned sales of dual-use products, amid bilateral tensions.

title: Japan reduces cost of ten-year passport to 8,900 yen
summary: The new fee represents a significant decrease, and in Kumamoto residents can obtain it for only 900 yen if they meet certain requirements.

title: 30-year prison sentence for perpetrator of deadly fire in Aichi
summary: The Nagoya court handed down the sentence for the crime of arson and homicide of three people in an apartment building.

title: Trump announces agreement with Iran to stop attacks and meeting on June 30
summary: The US president assured that both parties agreed to cease hostilities and hold talks.

title: Controversial statements on imperial succession reignite debate in Japan
summary: Former ministers and politicians claim that Princess Aiko cannot inherit the throne, sparking criticism and corrections.

title: Police ask for help to locate hit-and-run suspect in Tokyo
summary: Fugitive Hatta is believed to be in the Kanto area, four years after the incident.

title: Mother of prostituted Thai girl sentenced to 7 and a half years in prison
summary: The woman was found guilty of human trafficking and sexual exploitation of her 12-year-old daughter.

title: Naomi Osaka advances past first round of Wimbledon for third consecutive year
summary: The Japanese player advanced to the second round alongside Shintaro Mochizuki in the London grass tournament.

title: Opposition denounces forced approval of seat reduction law in Parliament
summary: The ruling party used its majority to initiate proceedings without consensus, which has stalled other initiatives.

title: Fuji TV and Yomiuri presidents participate in government councils sparking controversy
summary: The presence of media executives in official meetings raises doubts about press independence from power.
